content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :   File                                         Date    :   March 24, 1978


                                                                    A-78-03-005

From :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Barbara Campbell [illegible]

Subject:


        At its March 7, 1978, meeting the Fair Political
Practices Commission decided that gifts of free tickets
to bona fide political campaign events are not reportable
or prohibited under the campaign disclosure, conflict of
interest or lobbyist provisions of the Political Reform
Act.  The exclusion is only applicable to tickets given
and received as gifts to bona fide political campaign
events.

        The payments received from purchasers of tickets
as well as any payments that the sponsoring committee
makes in connection with the bona fide political campaign
events will have to be reported by the sponsoring committee.

        Lobbyists would continue to be prohibited from
purchasing tickets to a bona fide political campaign
event sponsored by a state candidate, elected state officer
or committee supporting either a state candidate or elected
state officer.




BC:plh

sections.conclusion: At its March 7, 1978, meeting the Fair Political Practices Commission decided that gifts of free tickets to bona fide political campaign events are not reportable or prohibited under the campaign disclosure, conflict of interest or lobbyist provisions of the Political Reform Act. The exclusion is only applicable to tickets given and received as gifts to bona fide political campaign events. The payments received from purchasers of tickets as well as any payments that the sponsoring committee makes in connection with the bona fide political campaign events will have to be reported by the sponsoring committee. Lobbyists would continue to be prohibited from purchasing tickets to a bona fide political campaign event sponsored by a state candidate, elected state officer or committee supporting either a state candidate or elected state officer.
sections.question_synthetic: Are gifts of free tickets to bona fide political campaign events reportable or prohibited under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Free tickets to bona fide political campaign events are not reportable or prohibited as gifts, but ticket payments and sponsoring committee expenditures must be reported. Lobbyists remain prohibited from purchasing such tickets.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional QUESTION section header; legal question inferred from the FPPC's substantive response. Recipient is Barbara Campbell. No explicit section headers found but conclusion content is clearly stated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are gifts of free tickets to bona fide political campaign events reportable or prohibited under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: At its March 7, 1978, meeting the Fair Political Practices Commission decided that gifts of free tickets to bona fide political campaign events are not reportable or prohibited under the campaign disclosure, conflict of interest or lobbyist provisions of the Political Reform Act. The exclusion is only applicable to tickets given and received as gifts to bona fide political campaign events. The payments received from purchasers of tickets as well as any payments that the sponsoring committee makes in connection with the bona fide political campaign events will have to be reported by the sponsoring committee. Lobbyists would continue to be prohibited from purchasing tickets to a bona fide political campaign event sponsored by a state candidate, elected state officer or committee supporting either a state candidate or elected state officer.

The exclusion is only applicable to tickets given and received as gifts to bona fide political campaign events. The payments received from purchasers of tickets as well as any payments that the sponsoring committee makes in connection with the bona fide political campaign events will have to be reported by the sponsoring committee. Lobbyists would continue to be prohibited from purchasing tickets to a bona fide political campaign event sponsored by a state candidate, elected state officer or committee supporting either a state candidate or elected state officer. embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that gifts of free tickets to bona fide political campaign events are not reportable or prohibited, though payments for tickets and related committee expenditures must be reported, and lobbyists are prohibited from purchasing such tickets.
